content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob, once again, play a game on an array.

Alice has an array AAA containing NNN positive integers.

The game proceeds for N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 turns. On the iii-th turn, the following occurs:


First, Alice sorts the array AAA in non-decreasing order.
Then, she tells Bob two integers PiP_iPi‚Äč and QiQ_iQi‚Äč (1‚Č§Pi<Qi‚Č§N+1‚ąíi1 \leq P_i \lt Q_i \leq N+1-i1‚Č§Pi‚Äč<Qi‚Äč‚Č§N+1‚ąíi).
Bob then has to choose **exactly one** of (APi+AQi)(A_{P_i}+A_{Q_i})(APi‚Äč‚Äč+AQi‚Äč‚Äč) or ‚ą£APi‚ąíAQi‚ą£|A_{P_i}-A_{Q_i}|‚ą£APi‚Äč‚Äč‚ąíAQi‚Äč‚Äč‚ą£.

Let Bob's chosen value be XXX.
Alice deletes APiA_{P_i}APi‚Äč‚Äč and AQiA_{Q_i}AQi‚Äč‚Äč from AAA, and appends XXX to it.
For example, if A=[3,12,5,24,1,3]A = [3, 12, 5, 24, 1, 3]A=[3,12,5,24,1,3]:


Alice sorts AAA to obtain A=[1,3,3,5,12,24]A = [1, 3, 3, 5, 12, 24]A=[1,3,3,5,12,24].
Then, say she gives indices 333 and 555 to Bob.
Bob can either choose A3+A5=15A_3 + A_5 = 15A3‚Äč+A5‚Äč=15, or ‚ą£A3‚ąíA5‚ą£=9|A_3 - A_5| = 9‚ą£A3‚Äč‚ąíA5‚Äč‚ą£=9 to append to the array.

In the first case, the array changes to [1,3,5,24,15][1, 3, 5, 24, 15][1,3,5,24,15].
In the second case, the array changes to [1,3,5,24,9][1, 3, 5, 24, 9][1,3,5,24,9].


The game then proceeds to the next turn.
Note that the length of the array decreases by 111 after each operation, so after all the turns are done AAA will contain a single integer.

Bob's aim is to **minimize** this final remaining value.

You are given the initial array AAA, as well as the sequences PPP and QQQ denoting the indices chosen by Alice on her moves.

Find **any** sequence of moves for Bob that minimizes the final value.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the length of AAA.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers ‚ÄĒ A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The iii-th of the next N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines will contain 222 space-separated integers PiP_iPi‚Äč and QiQ_iQi‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines corresponding to the moves you make.

The iii-th of these N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines should contain the number that gets inserted back into the array; which should equal either (APi+AQi)(A_{P_i} + A_{Q_i})(APi‚Äč‚Äč+AQi‚Äč‚Äč) or ‚ą£APi‚ąíAQi‚ą£|A_{P_i} - A_{Q_i}|‚ą£APi‚Äč‚Äč‚ąíAQi‚Äč‚Äč‚ą£ (after AAA has been sorted).


If there are multiple possible sequences of moves for Bob that minimize the final value, you may print any of them.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 750750750.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: A=[7,57]A = [7, 57]A=[7,57], Bob's best option is to choose ‚ą£7‚ąí57‚ą£=50|7-57| = 50‚ą£7‚ąí57‚ą£=50.
Test case 222: One sequence of optimal moves for Bob given Alice's choices is as follows:

First, the array is A=[3,4,5,6]A = [3, 4, 5, 6]A=[3,4,5,6] after sorting; Alice chooses indices 333 and 444.

Bob inserts ‚ą£A3‚ąíA4‚ą£=‚ą£5‚ąí6‚ą£=1|A_3-A_4| = |5-6| = 1‚ą£A3‚Äč‚ąíA4‚Äč‚ą£=‚ą£5‚ąí6‚ą£=1 into AAA.


Next, the array is A=[1,3,4]A = [1 ,3, 4]A=[1,3,4] after sorting; Alice chooses indices 222 and 333.

Bob inserts ‚ą£A2‚ąíA3‚ą£=‚ą£3‚ąí4‚ą£=1|A_2-A_3| = |3-4|=1‚ą£A2‚Äč‚ąíA3‚Äč‚ą£=‚ą£3‚ąí4‚ą£=1 into AAA.


Finally, the array is A=[1,1]A = [1, 1]A=[1,1]; Alice chooses indices 111 and 222.

Bob inserts ‚ą£A1‚ąíA2‚ą£=0|A_1-A_2|=0‚ą£A1‚Äč‚ąíA2‚Äč‚ą£=0 into AAA.
